column1: # mapping_set_title: CVE to NIST NVD LinkML schema mappings
#   Element-level mappings between the lmodel CVE schema (https://w3id.org/lmodel/cve)
#   and the lmodel NIST NVD schema (https://w3id.org/lmodel/nist-nvd). NVD enriches
#   raw CVE records with workflow status, version-agnostic CVSS metric views, CPE
#   applicability, and weakness attributions, so most mappings are closeMatch (NVD
#   side adds analyst-provided fields) or narrowMatch (CVE has version-specific

column7: NVDEntry wraps a CVE Record and adds NVD-only enrichment (workflow status, analyst metrics, CPE applicability) - same primary entity, broader scope.
Both group one or more CVSS scoring objects with format and scenario metadata.
NVD models CVSS as a single version-agnostic class with a CVSSVersion enum; CVE has a version-specific class.
NVD models CVSS as a single version-agnostic class with a CVSSVersion enum; CVE has a v3.x-specific class.
NVD models CVSS as a single version-agnostic class with a CVSSVersion enum; CVE has a v2.0-specific class.
Both model external references attached to a vulnerability; NVD adds reference-resource tags.
Both model CPE-based applicability statements describing affected configurations.
Identical concept and field semantics in both schemas - a single CPE match expression with optional version bounds.
Both attach a CWE weakness classification to a CVE; NVD adds source/scoring attributions.
Both identify the organization that produced a record or score; not the same shape but adjacent concepts.
Inherited from vulnerability_core in both schemas; same identifier, same pattern.
